title: mu-tui: per-session permissions UI + RAII attenuation flow + role picker
description:
## Problem

When a session is started, there's no UX for selecting its permission
profile. Substrate work for capabilities is being built (mu-hh1 adds
SupervisorCapability axis; mu-7rk-* adds auth + capability decomposition)
but the session-start UX layer is separate.

User wants RAII-style attenuation: sessions start with their initial
capability set and can be further attenuated (never expanded) over their
lifetime. Initial capabilities chosen at session-start via a picker with
sensible defaults.

## Proposal

### Session-start permission picker

When creating a new session (e.g., `n` key in F3 list):

- Default capability set highlighted (configurable per repo / user pref)
- `n + enter` to accept the default and start
- `j/k` or arrow keys to navigate predefined roles (e.g., "read-only",
  "writer", "tool-use", "delegated-supervisor")
- After role selection, enter starts session with that capability set

### RAII-style attenuation during session lifetime

- Active sessions can have their capabilities further restricted (never
  granted new ones)
- UI affordance for "attenuate this session" — picker showing current cap
  set with toggle-off for each
- Once attenuated, cannot be un-attenuated (must spawn a new session)

### Role-based defaults

- Roles defined in config or mu's admin surface
- Picker shows them in order: default first, then by frequency
- User can override default per session

## Acceptance

- New-session flow shows permission picker with default highlighted
- Default-acceptance path is fast (n+enter)
- Role navigation via j/k or arrows
- Active sessions can be attenuated via UI; not expanded
- Attenuation reflected immediately in what session can do

## Cross-refs

- mu-hh1 (SupervisorCapability axis — substrate for this UI)
- mu-7rk-* (auth + capability decomposition — substrate)
- mu-fnn (capability enforcement gate — must land before UI is fully
  meaningful)
- Provenance: TUI feedback (mu daemon f3845422 session-2, 2026-05-15)

title: mu-vha-tests: response-shape locking tests for auth protocol types (follow-up to mu-vha)
description:
Follow-up to mu-vha — add response-shape locking tests for the auth protocol types before mu-yox starts building handlers against them.

## Why

Codex Stage 2 review of mu-vha (verdict: ship_with_minor_changes, no blockers) flagged 3 important test-coverage gaps that should be closed before subsequent beads consume these types:

> "I would not block the bead, but the tests are thinner than advertised: response shapes and response unknown-field rejection need to be pinned down before later beads build handlers against this protocol surface."

The implementation is correct. The TESTS don't lock the wire shape tightly enough — a future accidental change to internal/external tagging or `deny_unknown_fields` behavior would silently break clients.

## Scope (additive only — no implementation changes)

### In

- **Exact-JSON shape tests for `AuthExchangeResponse` all three variants:**
  - `accepted`: `{"outcome": "accepted", "granted_capability": ...}`
  - `denied`: `{"outcome": "denied", "code": "invalid_credentials", "reason": "..."}`
  - `continue`: `{"outcome": "continue", "server_state_id": "...", "challenge": "..."}`
  - Plus roundtrip test for each variant
- **`AuthOfferResponse` tests:**
  - Exact JSON: `{"mechanisms": ["bearer", "gssapi"]}` (mixing known + Other variant)
  - Roundtrip with `Other` mechanism
  - Unknown-field rejection (e.g., `{"mechanisms": [...], "extra": true}` should fail)
- **`deny_unknown_fields` for `AuthExchangeResponse` variant payloads:**
  - Denied variant with extra field: `{"outcome": "denied", "code": "invalid_credentials", "reason": "x", "extra": true}` should fail to deserialize
  - Same for accepted and continue variants
- **`AuthMechanism` deserialize tests:**
  - `json!("bearer")` → `AuthMechanism::Bearer` (currently missing — codex minor #4)

### Out

- Any implementation changes (just tests)
- Validation logic for `AuthMechanism::Other(s)` accepting empty/uppercase strings (separate concern, mentioned in codex minor #1 — could file as separate bead if needed)
- Documentation updates (codex minor #2/#3 are doc tweaks; do separately if at all)

## Acceptance

- All new tests pass
- `cargo test --workspace` green; total count grows by exactly the number of new tests added
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
- `cargo fmt --check` clean
- No code change in `mu_core::protocol` outside `#[cfg(test)]` — types are stable
- mu-yox PR can rely on these types' wire shape being pinned by tests

## References

- mu-vha PR: #44 (https://github.com/sahuagin/mu/pull/44)
- Codex Stage 2 review for mu-vha: `~/.claude-personal/experiments/codex-review-mu-vha-output.txt` — "important_issues" #1, #2, #3 and "minor_issues" #4
- Blocks: should land before mu-yox (mu-7rk-b) starts implementation, OR before mu-yox PR opens
- Parent: part of mu-7rk decomposition chain

title: wall-clock and session-time injection into agent context
description:
Cheapest high-leverage time feature. Inject current wall-clock time and session-start time into context at every prompt submission. ~20 tokens per turn.

Fixes:
- Agent claims 'today' or 'this morning' without knowing session age
- Can't reason about 'it is late, don't suggest big refactors'
- Can't time-bound estimates
- Loop detection: 'you tried this 3 turns ago, same args, same result'

Implementation: UserPromptSubmit hook injects a system note with current time + session elapsed.

Phase 2 additions:
- Loop/repetition detection ('you tried this 3 turns ago')
- Stale-context warnings ('session running 4 hours, consider compaction')

From Opus mu-solo session (2026-05-27). Could land before 6/15.
close_reason: Landed in commit 5cd6b2bb. Appends 'Current time: HH:MM UTC. Session has been running for N minutes.' to the system prompt before every provider call. ~20 tokens per turn. Phase 2 (loop detection, stale-context warnings) deferred.

title: specs/architecture/session-lifecycle.md — session liveness as projection, reconstitution rules, handoff modes
description:
Capture the architectural framing surfaced 2026-05-20 in conversation between tcovert and Claude Opus (claude-personal): sessions have no active/inactive state at the substrate layer — there are only event logs and capability checks against their current state. "Active" is itself a projection (`LiveProjection`), not a property. This dissolves the handoff/resumption complexity by reducing all three cases (handoff-to-self, handoff-to-spawn, handoff-after-arbitrary-pause) to "make a new session with context + provenance, re-establish authority via the event log."

## Deliverable

`specs/architecture/session-lifecycle.md` (NEW) covering:

1. **Framing**: the substrate-thinking move applied to session liveness.
2. **Thesis**: no aliveness state; authority is established by capability check against current event-log state.
3. **LiveProjection**: rules for "session is live iff" — derived projection that powers TUI's Live sessions panel AND autonomous-loop heartbeat AND any other consumer that wants "what's happening right now."
4. **Reconstitution**: two-layer authority check at resume time (descriptor authority + runtime authority). Authority-to-read ≠ authority-to-act.
5. **Handoff modes**: parent-pointer (cheap, parent log accessible) vs copy-with-origin (durable, parent log may be archived/GC'd). Per-handoff choice encoded in the handoff event payload.
6. **Model migration as event**: `ModelMigrated { from, to, reason }` event variant; per-turn provider/model identity tracked in events. Resumption-across-migration is structurally identical to resumption within same model.
7. **Bootstrap**: root authority comes from outside the log (OS user, Biscuit root capsule, etc.). The log records authority *use*, not the source of root authority.
8. **Capsicum granularity is layering-independent**: the spec works whether mu-server is one-process-many-sessions (centralized app-level checks) or per-session-capsicum-sandboxed-workers (OS-enforced descriptor isolation). The choice is operational, not architectural.
9. **Invariants**: testable contracts that auditor checklists verify (INV-LIV-1 through INV-LIV-6).
10. **Follow-on work**: explicit pointers to mu-d033 (/handoff event + handoff-mode choice), seams.md (the tool-authority seam is the natural enforcement point), the `ModelMigrated` event variant, and the `LiveProjection` implementation.

## Acceptance

- File exists at `specs/architecture/session-lifecycle.md`.
- All six invariants are stated in directly-testable form.
- Cross-references to `event-sourced-context.md`, `capability-delegation.md`, `mu-capability-substrate.md`.
- Follow-on bead pointers wired (this bead becomes the closing artifact; implementation beads can reference it).

## Why this matters now

The seams suite (mu-ml6m and family) describes the SHAPE of multi-agent code review. This spec describes the SHAPE of session lifetime — what's preserved, what's projected, what's reconstitutable. The two complement: the tool-authority seam in seams.md enforces what this spec mandates (Capability checks always against current event-log state, never against aliveness).

## References

- Conversation: claude-personal session 2026-05-20 → 2026-05-21 (after the daemon ebccc9 session-2 framework writeup)
- Memory: substrate_thinking_architectural_moves_2026_05_20 (the architectural lens)
- Memory: mu_posiwid_cat_for_agents_2026_05_20 (auditor-readability that this spec serves)
- Memory: multi_agent_review_distribution_seams_framework_2026_05_20 (the complementary code-review framework)
- Related beads: mu-ml6m (seams.md), mu-d033 (/handoff event), mu-7lwn (auditor capability)

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text:
2026-05-22 (daily-driver session): Operator naming preference — bake this vocabulary into the lifecycle state model when drafting session-lifecycle.md:

- **ALIVE**: attached to a running session (matches the existing `LiveProjection: true` framing)
- **MUMMIFIED**: archived intentionally with metadata preserved; expects to be dusted off and continued. Cheap to revive.
- **FOSSILIZED**: deeply archived; requires the reanimate process to bring back. Expensive to revive (may involve event-log rehydration, capability re-establishment, model-migration).
- **REANIMATED**: previously dormant session restored to ALIVE (Re-Animator film reference). "Reconstitution" in current draft remains the architectural term; reanimate is the user-facing verb.
- **DUST OFF**: operation moving MUMMIFIED → ALIVE without compaction (cheap revival path).

The six-action rewind/reanimate picker (filed as **mu-79iu** depending on this spec) consumes this state model; the spec should enumerate state transitions explicitly so the picker implementation has a contract.

Memory cross-ref: [[humor_cultural_callbacks_in_code_naming]] `42b06542` — Re-Animator pattern fits the established convention (DoubleTap, SplinelTap).

title: TaskTelemetry: per-tier cache-write split (ephemeral_5m/1h input tokens)
description:
WHY: PR #178 made cache TTL per-session (solo default 1h @ 2.0x write premium, workers pinned 5m @ 1.25x). But mu's own telemetry only records cache_write_tokens TOTAL — the dashboard/cost formulas INFER the tier from config instead of PROVING it from the API response. Anthropic returns the split in usage.cache_creation.{ephemeral_5m_input_tokens,ephemeral_1h_input_tokens}; claude-code transcripts already carry it (mu_stats.sql parses it on the cc leg). mu's events are the blind spot. Identified in the 2026-06-04/05 economics wave readout.

WHAT (field names LOCKED — the stats layer is being extended against these in parallel):
1. crates/mu-ai/src/providers/anthropic.rs — AnthropicUsage (line ~693) parses only flat cache_creation_input_tokens. Add nested struct for cache_creation.{ephemeral_5m_input_tokens,ephemeral_1h_input_tokens}; handle BOTH the non-streaming response and the streaming usage merge (~line 825); preserve existing total field semantics.
2. EventPayload::TaskTelemetry (crates/mu-core/src/event_log.rs:370) — add OPTIONAL fields cache_write_5m_tokens / cache_write_1h_tokens (#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]) — additive, no migration, old events deserialize unchanged.
3. Thread provider usage -> telemetry emission (crates/mu-coding/src/serve/forwarder.rs:469) through whatever shared usage struct sits between them (trace it).
4. STRETCH (in scope if clean): pricing — when the split is present, cost writes at 1.25x (5m) / 2.0x (1h) instead of flat-rate assumption; fall back to current behavior when absent. Check crates/mu-core/src/pricing.rs.

OUT OF SCOPE: openrouter (returns cache_creation_input_tokens: None today), openai (no write premium), backfilling old events.

TESTS: wire fixture with both tiers nonzero; streaming merge keeps tiers; TaskTelemetry serde roundtrip incl. None-skipping; existing tests untouched.

STOP GOALS: (1) fresh mu session emits nonzero cache_write_1h_tokens under solo defaults; (2) tiers sum to cache_write_tokens total on fixtures; (3) cargo test + just ci-aipr green.

RELATED: mu-mucm (stats layer consumes these), mu-f1a0 (#178 created the need), mu-8bkf (next wave reads the resulting numbers).
close_reason: done

THREE SEAMS (sized ~a day total, no architectural novelty):

SCANNER (console/cc_data.rs): read ~/.claude-personal/projects/*/*.jsonl, map the cc wrapper shape (type: assistant/user envelopes, message.usage, message.model, timestamps, session id from filename/sessionId) into SessionSummary rows — provider 'claude-code', daemon column carries the project dir. Index shows both corpora in one table. Parse reference: the DuckDB decode in ~/src/claude-personal/scripts/t4c_tool_inventory.sql (read_ndjson_objects + content[*] unnest) is the proven map of the cc schema; this seam is its Rust translation.
2. DETAIL VIEW: cc content blocks -> existing transcript_block rendering (user/assistant/tool map cleanly); cost tab works from message.usage per assistant turn.

Mark store = task_log sidecar: tag session-mark, session_ref = cc session id (column exists, unused). Console mark POST grows a second storage path keyed by provider. Related: mu-operator-mark-5mwr (event + capture, closed), mu-index-mark-column-auiv (index stars), mu-mucm (projection layer / dashboards).

description:

Status update 2026-05-11 evening: user has been successfully completing both openai-codex and anthropic-api calls in the past few hours; the 14hr stall observed 2026-05-10/11 seems to have resolved vendor-side. Not closing — stalls in cloud-API layers can recur intermittently and the right thing is to keep watching.

mu-035 Phase B (periodic heartbeat) was designed specifically to prevent silent hangs like the one that motivated this bead — if/when a stall recurs, the heartbeat ticks should make it visible in F8 firehose almost immediately, and session.cancel_outstanding (mu-035 Phase C) provides a clean abort path. Cross-filed as a new monitoring task to keep codex in regular rotation so we'd notice a recurrence within a day rather than weeks.

title: TUI: unify F1 + F2 as collapse/expand of one session view
description:
## Reframing (2026-05-18)

Original framing (2026-05-11): F2 = standalone session-tree pane with unicode box-drawing.

Updated framing: F1 and F2 are not separate panes — they are **collapse/expand modes of the same view**. F1 is the collapsed flat list (today's Live Sessions panel). F2 is the same view expanded: each session row reveals child sessions (via session.list parent_session_id) plus per-session properties (mibs) underneath, formatted readably by default with a key to copy raw mib form.

This means one panel, one selection model, one rendering tree — F1/F2 just toggle the per-row expansion state. Avoids the navigation friction of two parallel session views that always need re-syncing.

## Behavior

- **F1 (default)**: flat list of sessions, one row per session, status icon + cost + token count (today's rendering).
- **F2**: same panel, every row expanded by default. Children rendered indented under parent via unicode box-drawing (per original mockup). Selected-row property section renders cost / token-count / model / provider / capabilities / parent-id / etc. in readable form.
- **Per-row expand toggle**: a key (likely Enter on F1, or some chord on F2) toggles expansion of just that row. Lets you have mostly-collapsed F1 with a few expanded children visible.
- **Mib copy**: a key (e.g. ‘y’ for yank) on a focused row exports its full mib form to clipboard for grep / paste / debugging.
- **Tree built client-side** from session.list parent_session_id (no new RPC needed).

## Example expanded form (from mu-ui-mockups-2026-05-10.md)

```
root: mu product discussion
├─ impl: mu-022 edit tool        ● run
│  ├─ delegate: tests            ✓ done
│  └─ review                     ○ wait
├─ design: event context         ✓ done
└─ design: UI mockups            ● here
```

Each row has hidden mib details below it; expanding (per-row) shows the formatted properties.

## Acceptance

- One panel handles both F1 and F2 modes
- F2 keypress = expand all; F1 keypress = collapse all
- Per-row Enter or similar toggles individual expansion
- Tree rendering uses session.list parent_session_id (client-side build)
- Mib copy key exports raw mib form
- Selection model survives expand/collapse transitions

## Composes with

- mu-770 (archive/restore) — archived sessions filter out of F1/F2 default; toggle shows them
- mu-pmh (per-session control surface) — selected row's actions go through here
- mu-bs8 (per-session permissions UI) — capability info is part of the mib properties displayed on expand

## Out of scope

- Multi-pane comparison (split-screen two sessions side by side) — separate UX problem
- Mib-edit (only read/copy in v1)

title: Multi-breakpoint prompt caching: mu's single cache boundary leaves ~45% of session cost on the table
description:
MEASURED (2026-06-04 harness comparison, same 15-prompt workload, sonnet): claude-code's session ran 110 uncached input tokens TOTAL (multi-breakpoint cache discipline); mu's sonnet replay ran 607,865 — $1.82 of its $4.08. mu's AnthropicCacheStrategy places ONE ephemeral boundary (context_assembly.cache_boundary_count=1, anthropic.rs:521/530 emit two cache_control marks: system + one message boundary); everything after the boundary re-bills at full input rate every call.

FIX: place up to 4 breakpoints (Anthropic max) — e.g. system/kernel, stable prefix, mid-conversation, near-tail — so the uncached suffix per call shrinks to just the newest spans. Est. effect on the replay workload: $4.08 -> ~$2.60 (-36%), compounding with mu-f1a0 (1h TTL).

Evidence: ~/.claude-personal/experiments/replay-economics-2026-06-04.md (harness comparison section); driver logs ~/mu-replays/replay-claude-code-20260604-215201.jsonl vs replay-anthropic_api-20260604-164334.jsonl. Related: mu-f1a0 (TTL), mu-2a6u (double-pay).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #177: conversation spans cacheable (assembly.rs), four-anchor AnthropicCacheStrategy (system/intro-end/pre-turn/rope-end, capped at uncacheable holes), wire emission for marked conversation blocks. Benchmark replay to measure vs ~$2.60 prediction follows.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text: BENCHMARK MEASURED (post-merge, 2026-06-04 23:19 run): uncached input 607,865 -> 96 tokens (beats claude-code's 110); cache_boundary_count 1 -> 3. Total $3.98 vs predicted ~$2.60 — but the run made 66 model calls vs the prior run's 38 (sonnet elaborated; output 27.1K vs 16.3K). Per-call cost $0.107 -> $0.060 (-44%), matching the prediction at equal work; counterfactual for this run's shape at pre-chiw rates ~$7.09. Durable: events/7ace69dc0c21dcf9/. FOLLOW-UP: boundaries peak at 3, never 4 — pre_turn anchor appears to dedup into a neighbor more than geometry suggests; check whether last-User detection sees the live rope's span kinds as expected.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text: FOLLOW-UP RESOLVED (same night): the 'missing 4th boundary' is not a dedup bug. Replay sessions are created WITHOUT a system_prompt, and the intro's recall spans are SpanKind::MemoryInjection / FileLoad (assembly.rs:113-118), not System — so system_idx=None and 3 boundaries is correct for this rope shape. Sessions with a system prompt get all 4. OPTIONAL future micro-opt: when system_idx is None, the spare slot could fund a second pre-turn anchor (two turns back) for deeper re-match resilience. Not filed as work — noting only.

title: Faux provider spec: pin design constraints so it can't drift out from under nextest
description:
## Why

crates/mu-ai/src/providers/faux.rs is 184 lines and is critical infrastructure — it's what makes 'cargo nextest run' cost zero. But there's no spec calling out the design constraints (Provider trait subset it covers, behaviors it deliberately mocks, behaviors it deliberately bombs out on). Without a spec, a future Provider-trait method addition could quietly leave faux not-usefully-implementing it.

## Action

specs/architecture/faux-provider.md (or similar) — short doc capturing: faux is a test fixture, not a degraded production provider; every Provider trait method must have a faux impl that's either useful for tests or a panic with a clear message; new Provider methods are gated on faux coverage.

## Source

opus-4-7 review event 194, 'What needs work' item 8.

title: Durability contract mismatch: docs say JSONL is write-ahead source of truth; code is best-effort (failures logged and ignored)
description: Found by self-hosted audit session 013ff06b4b86046b (2026-06-03, 'are architecture and code out of sync?'), verified against main@761c5e7f. ~/CLAUDE.md + architecture docs state 'write-ahead, not write-back: no event exists in memory that isn't already durable on disk.' Code (crates/mu-core/src/event_log.rs:512-514, mu-upb): 'best-effort JSONL write before the in-memory push... failures are logged and ignored — disk persistence is not load-bearing.' If a disk write fails, memory holds events absent from the JSONL — rehydration and any log-derived analysis silently lose them. DECISION NEEDED: either (a) harden code (fail/abort the append on disk-write failure, making the WAL claim true), or (b) soften the docs to 'write-first, best-effort'. Given the event log is increasingly load-bearing (rehydration mu-u1ld, ContextAssembly/CompactionAssembly forensics from PR #161), (a) seems right, but it changes failure semantics on full disks.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text: Operator decision (2026-06-03): make durability behavior CONFIGURABLE. Default = continue on disk-write failure but RAISE AN ALERT to the user (current behavior is continue + tracing::warn only, invisible in-session). Strict mode (fail/stop accepting events on write failure) available for deployments that need the WAL guarantee — operator's example: a trading shop would want to halt rather than continue un-journaled. Implementation sketch: [event_log] on_write_failure = "continue-alert" | "halt" (default continue-alert); alert path should surface in-session (callout event / session.callout), not just daemon logs. Docs then describe both modes honestly (fixes the 'write-ahead source of truth' overclaim).

title: compaction: no compaction occurred (or was evented) at 616K input despite heuristic/150K config
description: Harvest from self-hosted session 3e81075bc9a54d0f/session-1 (2026-06-03, openai_codex/gpt-5.5, 12 turns, 28 min). Operator config: [compaction] default_policy=heuristic, trigger_threshold_tokens=150000. Per-turn provider-reported input_tokens hit 128K, 209K, and 616K (416K cache-read) — three turns over the trigger — yet the event log contains zero compaction-related events (kinds seen: provider_status_update, context_assembly, assistant_message_event, tool_call, tool_result, user_message, done, task_telemetry, session_created). event_log.rs module doc mentions Compaction events but no payload kind appeared. INVESTIGATE: (a) does the heuristic policy's token measure (assembled-context estimate) diverge from provider-reported input (codex counts reasoning + cache-read)? (b) did compaction run but not get evented (eventing gap)? (c) is the policy wired for the openai_codex provider path at all? Repro material: ~/.local/share/mu/events/3e81075bc9a54d0f/session-1.jsonl. Also note context_assembly events carry no token estimate field — attribution gap, related mu-u6hc/mu-ppyi.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text: Investigation cluster: open bug mu-8bkf (live compaction no-op by default; hash-and-summary not wired to serve path — note operator config DOES set default_policy=heuristic, so 8bkf's default-resolution clause alone doesn't explain the no-fire) + memory 44737121 (2026-05-27 CRITICAL: heuristic compaction corrupts tool history — evicts tool spans, leaves dangling tool_use refs, provider 400s; possibly why wiring was left off). Operator theory: 'guessing it's not wired.' Deadline context: wants this looked at before 17:00 (theorized degraded-performance window).

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text:
RESOLVED (2026-06-03 16:33 investigation): compaction correctly did NOT fire — the trigger was never crossed. The 128K/209K/616K figures are per-TURN SUMS of input_tokens across model calls (done.usage aggregates; verified: every done.usage.input_tokens == sum of that turn's assistant_message_event usages, e.g. 616,381 = 13 calls of ≤51,194 each). Max SINGLE-call provider input all session: 51,374 tokens — a third of the 150K threshold. mu's own trigger measure (tiktoken over assembled rope, loop_/mod.rs:967) would be even lower (~25K content estimate at session end).

Answers to the bead's three questions:
(a) divergence — yes, but the bigger issue is the bead's premise: done/task_telemetry usage is a per-turn billing aggregate, not a context size. Per-call input (the context-like number) topped out at 51K.
(b) eventing gap — REAL but moot here: forwarder.rs:686 to_log_event maps CompactionAssembly => None, so the durable log structurally cannot record compaction even when it fires (mu-za92). Absence of compaction events proves nothing.
(c) wiring — VERIFIED PRESENT for serve (and mu-solo spawns mu serve as child): session.rs:301 resolve_compaction_policy("heuristic") => Some, passed as compaction_policy_override + compaction_threshold into AgentConfig. Provider-independent in the loop.

Bonus findings: (1) scripts/deep-analyze.py:162-168 double-counts context for codex logs (sums input+cache_read, but OpenAI input_tokens already INCLUDES cached; correct only for Anthropic accounting) — its "5 likely compactions" on this session are spurious cache-miss turns. Separate bead filed. (2) The heuristic tool-history corruption (memory 44737121) was fixed in 81a18e5 "fix(compaction): drop assistant tool_use spans with their tool clusters" — armed heuristic config is now safe. (3) The 17:00 degraded-performance theory is unsupported by this session: context never exceeded ~51K per call.

Recommend: close as invalid-premise; the real residue is mu-za92 (CompactionAssembly durability) + the new deep-analyze accounting bead.

title: mu-core: consolidate scattered civil-time helpers into one module (days_from_civil + civil_from_days + rfc3339 parse/format)

Create one module (e.g. mu_core::time or a mu-coding-local time module if mu-core placement drags deps — implementer judges by import graph), move all four + their unit tests, point both call sites at it, delete the duplicates and the cross-referencing doc comments in favor of one canonical doc block. Round-trip property test (days_from_civil∘civil_from_days = id) is cheap and seals it. No behavior change; module-too-small-for-a-crate is the agreed scope.

title: claude-code harness: PreToolUse hook to JIT-inject tool-conventions for personal tools
description:
## Problem

Each new claude-code session rediscovers the same first-time errors with our
personal tools. Canonical case: `agent memory search "goal-protocol"` errors
with FTS5 `no such column: protocol` because hyphens aren't quoted; the rule
is already documented but isn't in the session prompt at start. Path is:
hit error → search memory → find convention → apply workaround. We pay
rediscovery cost every session, and the user observes the same first-call
failure happens "every time" across sessions.

Source of truth for these conventions lives at
`~/.claude-personal/skills/goal-protocol/references/tool-conventions.md`
(currently ~21k, multiple gotchas already captured), but it's only consulted
reactively after a failure.

## Proposal

PreToolUse hook on Bash, configured in `~/.claude-personal/settings.json`,
that pattern-matches the about-to-execute invocation and injects the
relevant convention JIT — before the call runs:

- `agent memory search` → FTS5 quote-hyphenated-terms note
- `br create`           → phantom-verify discipline + `--silent` chain pattern
- `br label add`        → `--label` (singular, not `--labels`)
- `pi --tools all`      → hangs note
- ...etc., one-to-one with sections in tool-conventions.md

Hook reads the relevant section of tool-conventions.md (or a derived JSON
map keyed by tool/pattern) and emits a short reminder. tool-conventions.md
remains the source of truth; adding a new convention = edit one file.

## Acceptance

- New claude-personal sessions don't hit the documented first-time errors
- Hook adds a convention reminder visible to the agent before the matched
  tool actually runs
- Hook overhead minimal (sub-50ms per matched call; non-matched calls
  unaffected)
- Adding a new convention requires only editing tool-conventions.md (or its
  derived map), no hook code changes for content updates

## Cross-refs

- `~/.claude-personal/skills/goal-protocol/references/tool-conventions.md`
- memory da1701b4 (agent memory search FTS5 bug)
- memory 666fa1b1 (goal-protocol methodology validation; references
  tool-conventions.md as load-bearing)
- memory 64b4a3a6 + a033efde (3-layer orchestration pattern; this hook
  improves the supervisor's first-touch ergonomics)

## Notes on filing location

Filed in mu's beads namespace because we'll be working in mu for months and
the goal-protocol exercise lives here, not because the change is mu code.
Implementation will land in `~/.claude-personal/settings.json` + possibly a
small helper script under `~/.claude-personal/`.

Implementation slices ordered: agent.sqlite schema first.

title: Cluster-service spec (mu-040): cross-machine federation
description:
Per memory d32898bd. Cross-machine mu visibility goes through a SEPARATE cluster-service process, not via etcd-backed-into-each-daemon.

Service responsibilities:
- Accept client connections (TUI, web UI, agents)
- Authentication / authorization
- Use etcd to enumerate sessions across all daemons in the cluster
- Aggregate cross-daemon mailbox routing

Daemons stay narrow: they just announce themselves + sessions to etcd via a thin SessionDiscovery::EtcdAnnouncerBackend (or similar). The aggregation happens elsewhere.

Future spec — write when we have a real driver for cross-machine work. Likely paired with biscuit-auth for the trust layer.

title: /handoff event + session-resume-from-event: cache-managed mid-session continuation
description:
## Idea (operator 2026-05-17)

Add a /handoff command (or prompt+tool exchange producing a Handoff event) that:

1. Captures a handoff message into the session event log — the message itself contains whatever state the next session needs (pointers to information to reuse, summary, current goal, etc.)
2. On restart of a session, the new instance can be pointed at that event and pick up cleanly without re-processing the whole prior context
3. Primary motivation is cache management — handoff is usually done because the prompt cache has gotten unwieldy or stale, so we want to checkpoint-then-restart with a smaller hot context

## Why this fits mu's architecture

- The event log is already the substrate (per specs/architecture/event-sourced-context.md). A Handoff event is just another EventPayload variant — or even just a specially-tagged assistant_message_event with content acting as the handoff payload.
- 'Session resume from event' is the projection: rebuild context from {SessionCreated → Handoff} rather than {SessionCreated → all_subsequent_events}.
- It's the mid-session analog of mu-mh4 'sessions persist across daemon restart' — same machinery, narrower scope (intra-day, intra-task), different motivation (cache reset vs daemon restart).

## Design questions

- **Event shape**: dedicated EventPayload::Handoff { summary, pointers: Vec<EventPointer>, goal: Option<String> } vs reuse assistant_message_event with a structural marker? The pointer-set framing (skill.rs:'activating a skill IS pointer-set membership') suggests the dedicated variant is cleaner.
- **Resume protocol**: new RPC session.resume_from_handoff { session_id, event_id }? Or session.create with optional 'resume_anchor: EventPointer'?
- **Cache implication**: the resume should produce a SMALLER prompt than the original context (that's the whole point). Provider-side caching invalidation is implicit — different prompt = different cache entry. Worth confirming the Anthropic provider clears its cache_breakpoints accordingly.

## Composes with

- mu-mh4 territory (persist sessions across daemon restart) — both depend on event-log-as-source-of-truth.
- /compaction (mu-kgu) — adjacent: compaction shrinks the rope in place; handoff is a structural restart that drops everything except the explicit handoff message. Operator may want both available, used for different reasons.
- Mailbox / cross-session pointers — a handoff message in session A could point at events in session B.

## Acceptance

- /handoff command (or equivalent) writes a structured event to the session log
- Resume mechanism reads that event and starts a new session with content seeded from the handoff
- Verifiable cache benefit on resume (next assistant turn shows cache_creation_input_tokens >> cache_read_input_tokens, i.e. fresh cache, not stale-prefix-reuse)

## Source

Operator message 2026-05-17 during opus-review session.

title: mu-solo: live-viewport streaming + nested collapsible concurrent turns (demux by session_id, commit-on-finalize)
description:
## Problem

mu-solo commits streaming text to linear terminal scrollback incrementally (`vp.insert_before` per delta; `streaming_chars_emitted` tracks how much is committed). Scrollback is append-only, so two concurrent streams (main turn + `/btw` sidecar) cannot interleave coherently — which is why `/btw` is guarded during streaming (app.rs ~753/799/982). The single streaming state (`streaming_text` / `streaming_header_open` / `streaming_chars_emitted` / `pending_close` / `streaming_route`) assumes exactly one in-flight turn.

The same incremental-commit model is the root of two open bugs:
- mu-304g: F3 inline render stalls ~20 lines into a streaming response.
- mu-926g: empty/unclosed assistant header artifacts in agent loops.

## Design (converged with operator, 2026-05-28)

ENGINE — live region, commit-on-finalize. Render in-flight turn(s) in a live, mutable viewport region (the `DynamicViewport` already manages a dynamic bottom area for the grow-up prompt) redrawn each tick from per-session buffers, and `insert_before` a block into scrollback only on `assistant_text_finalized`, then drop it from the live set. A live region redraws from scratch each frame, so N blocks can grow at once with no interleave problem. Commit blocks ordered by START timestamp so out-of-order finishes still read chronologically.

DEMUX by session_id. `handle_notification` (~app.rs:1904) already resolves each delta's session_id to main-vs-sidecar; route deltas into per-session StreamState (replace the single streaming_* fields). Then relax the /btw guard so it fires mid-stream.

LAYOUT — inline collapsible child events. Render a /btw as a timestamp-anchored, collapsible CHILD event inside the outer assistant block, generalizing the existing inline tool-call rendering (emit_tool_call_started/completed) so a model tool-call and a /btw aside become two flavors of one inline-collapsible-child-event model. Collapsed = +you / +assistant one-liners; expand to read. Preserves causal flow (you see where in the turn you stepped out).

LABELS as provenance (folds mu-za0u; roadmap #2). Replace the bare `assistant` label with session:provider:model (alias-able per mu-za0u; daemon hoisted to the status line via the shipped MCP status-line rail; full daemon:session:provider:model recoverable on expand / /status). The label IS the honesty layer: a /btw farmed to a different session/provider:model wears its origin (opus main + haiku aside reads truthfully). NOTE: a /btw is NOT the main model's tool call — it is an operator aside to the sidecar session, represented as a labeled operator digression, not a model tool_use.

COLLAPSE/EXPAND. ratatui in-pane overlay (Clear + bordered Block) for expand; same affordance serves long tool output (the Claude Code ctrl+o pattern). No external multiplexer needed.

## Phases

1. Engine: per-session StreamState + live-viewport rendering of in-flight turn(s); commit-on-finalize; timestamp-ordered commit. (Incidentally fixes mu-304g / mu-926g.)
2. Demux + unguard: route deltas by session_id; relax the /btw streaming guard.
3. Nested layout: inline collapsible timestamp-anchored child events; unify tool-calls + /btw.
4. Provenance labels (mu-za0u / #2): session:provider:model alias; daemon to status line.
5. Collapse/expand: ratatui overlay; reused for long tool output.

## Folds in / relates
- mu-304g (streaming stall ~20 lines) — fixed by Phase 1.
- mu-926g (empty/unclosed assistant header) — fixed by Phase 1.
- mu-za0u (model attribution / interleaved view) — implemented by Phase 4.

## Constraints
- mu-solo is single-pane by design (deliberate pivot off mu-tui's Inline/Fullscreen state machine; memory e16c24a6). This work stays in-pane (ratatui overlays). True separate panes = mu-6nuq, deferred.

## Provenance
Design converged in a mu-solo planning session 2026-05-28 (operator + claude). Covers roadmap items #1 (/btw runs during streaming) + #2 (model-attribution labels). The /btw fix had no prior bead; see memory 521c83c2 followup #2.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text:
PHASE 1 SCOPE DECISION (2026-05-28): go 'foundation', not the line-patch. Build the structured turn model + composable renderers now — it's what Phase 3 (nested collapsible /btw) requires anyway, so patching first then tearing out is the throwaway path.

CURRENT STATE (terrain): mu-solo has NO model/view split. It's immediate-mode imperative — a notification arrives, an emit_* fn builds Vec<Line> AND draws the box AND insert_before's it to scrollback in one step, then forgets it. No retained model, no component tree. Streaming text commits incrementally (emit_streaming -> emit_body_chunk per newline) = the mu-304g/926g cause.

DESIGN (immediate-mode; NO retained view objects / offsets / observer-command machinery — re-render the model each frame, ratatui paints):
- MODEL on App: in-flight Turn { route: TurnRoute, items: Vec<TurnItem> }. enum TurnItem = Text(String) | ToolCall{name,primary_arg} | ToolResult{outcome} (+ later Btw(Turn) for Phase 3 nesting). Phase 1: one in-flight turn; Phase 2 makes it a per-session map keyed by session_id.
- VIEW: pure composable fns in render.rs — render_turn(&Turn, wrap_width, max_rows)->Vec<Line> delegating to render_text_item / render_tool_call / render_tool_result. Extract today's emit_* / emit_body_chunk / block_lines line-building into these pure fns. Tail-truncate to max_rows for the live (in-flight) preview; emit closer only when committing.
- LIVE: render_viewport renders the in-flight Turn into the viewport above the prompt each frame (tail-truncated, no closer). focus_mode suppresses the preview (model still built + committed).
- handle_notification builds the MODEL: text_delta extends the trailing Text item; tool_call_started pushes ToolCall; tool_call_completed pushes ToolResult; assistant_text_finalized = segment marker; done/error -> render Turn with closer, insert_before to scrollback, drop the model.
- DELETE the incremental path (emit_streaming + immediate insert_before inside emit_tool_call_*; the logic moves into the pure item renderers). streaming_chars_emitted/pending_close/streaming_header_open retire in favor of model+render.

FIXES: mu-304g (no incremental scrollback commit -> no stall) + mu-926g (no eager empty header). SETS UP: Phase 2 (per-session map + demux + unguard /btw) and Phase 3 (Btw(Turn) item + collapse/expand). Execution is a clean focused run against this spec.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text:
Phase 1 progress (2026-05-28, claude-personal): foundation LANDED + verified green; daily-driver path untouched.

DONE (additive, zero-risk):
- render.rs: pure renderers render_turn / render_turn_item / render_tool_result / turn_closer / tail_truncate + TurnItem enum {Text, ToolCall{display_name,primary_arg}, ToolResult{kind,text}, Error}. Reproduce the existing visual contract (header / | body / bullet ToolName(arg) yellow-bold / |   bounded preview / red error / closer). 11 render:: tests pass incl. the codex-validated invariant 'tail_truncate(full) == tail of full render' (structural guard vs mu-304g).
- app.rs: Turn{route,items} + Turn::{new,push_text,has_output} + App.live_turn: Option<Turn> + ctor init. Builds green (expected dead_code warns until wired).

PRIOR ART: studied codex-rs/tui (now indexed). Confirms architecture (HistoryCell model + StreamController live region + insert_history commit). Keep ENUM TurnItem (codex trait only because ~12 cell types); keep Phase-1 commit-whole-turn-on-done (codex on_commit_tick = future opt, separate bead not P1).

REMAINING WIRING (daily-driver-mutating; do atomically + visual-verify in one run):
1. handle_notification -> build live_turn (text_delta->push_text; tool started/completed->push items, move titlecase_tool/extract_primary_arg to build-time; finalized->replace trailing Text per mu-wk2; done/error->render_turn+turn_closer, insert_before, drop live_turn).
2. render_viewport: render live_turn above separator each frame, tail-truncated; focus_mode suppresses preview (model still built+committed).
3. DELETE emit_streaming/emit_body_chunk/flush_streaming_close/ensure_streaming_header_open + streaming_text/header_open/chars_emitted/pending_close fields; fix cmd_btw guard (live_turn.is_some()) + handle_message phase-2 closer.
4. build+clippy+test; hand to thaddeus_user for visual check. Fixes mu-304g + mu-926g by construction.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text:
Phase 1 IMPLEMENTED + verified green (2026-05-28, claude-personal). Wiring complete, daily-driver path swapped to the structured model.

DONE:
- handle_notification builds the model: text_delta -> Turn::push_text (extends trailing Text item, new item after a tool call); assistant_text_finalized -> replace trailing Text with canonical (mu-wk2); tool_call_started -> push ToolCall (titlecase_tool/extract_primary_arg moved to build-time); tool_call_completed -> push ToolResult{kind,text}; done/error -> render_turn + turn_closer, insert_before to scrollback, drop live_turn (error attaches as trailing Error item; empty/no-turn -> standalone no-output/error block).
- render_viewport renders live_turn above the prompt each frame, tail-truncated to a budget that guarantees >=1 (up to 3) prompt rows stay visible; focus_mode suppresses the preview (model still built+committed).
- DELETED emit_streaming / emit_body_chunk / flush_streaming_close / ensure_streaming_header_open + fields streaming_text/streaming_header_open/streaming_chars_emitted/pending_close/ask_had_output/pending_skip_assistant (last was write-only dead code). Kept streaming_route (synced w/ live_turn.route; used by /cancel). Guards (Ctrl-C cancel, /btw, /skill) now check live_turn.is_some().
- net diff crates/mu-solo: +500/-495. cargo build + clippy clean; 32 mu-solo tests pass (incl render:: tail-invariant). Fixes mu-304g (no incremental scrollback commit -> no stall) + mu-926g (no eager empty header) by construction.

PENDING: operator visual TUI check in thaddeus_user workspace (live gpt-5.5 session). Uncommitted on disk (beads-ride). Phases 2-5 (per-session demux + /btw unguard, nested collapsible child events, provenance labels, collapse/expand overlay) remain.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text:
PHASE 2 SPEC — per-session demux + /btw unguard (2026-05-28, claude-personal, written warm right after Phase 1 shipped to main @ 9de8e4c3).

GOAL: let a /btw sidecar turn stream CONCURRENTLY with the main turn. Phase 1 built the substrate (structured Turn, live-region render, commit-on-done); Phase 2 generalizes the single in-flight turn to a per-session set and drops the guard that blocks /btw mid-stream.

== CURRENT STATE (post-Phase-1, crates/mu-solo/src/app.rs) ==
- Model: `App.live_turn: Option<Turn>` (single in-flight turn). `Turn { route: TurnRoute, items: Vec<render::TurnItem> }` + Turn::{new(route), push_text(delta), has_output()}. TurnRoute = Main | Btw (header_label/color/you_label/you_color).
- `streaming_route: Option<TurnRoute>` — single global, kept in sync with the live turn; used by cmd_cancel + as the route fallback in handlers.
- handle_notification: top resolves `let sid = params["session_id"]` and classifies main (== self.session_id) / sidecar (== self.sidecar_session_id) / unknown (drops with return). Arms: text_delta -> live_turn.get_or_insert_with(|| Turn::new(route)).push_text; assistant_text_finalized -> replace trailing Text item (mu-wk2); tool_call_started -> push TurnItem::ToolCall{display_name,primary_arg} (titlecase_tool/extract_primary_arg at build time); tool_call_completed -> push TurnItem::ToolResult{kind,text}; done|error -> `self.live_turn.take()`, commit (render::render_turn + render::turn_closer, insert_before) or standalone no-output/error block, then `streaming_route = None`.
- render_viewport (~line 583): builds `preview` from `self.live_turn.as_ref()` (skipped if focus_mode), tail-truncated to budget = MAX_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT - (4 + menu_rows + prompt_lines.min(3)); prepended to `lines` above the separator. VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=5, MAX_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=20.
- Guards keyed off `live_turn.is_some()`: Ctrl-C cancel (key handler), cmd_btw ("wait — main turn still streaming"), cmd_skill ("wait — turn still streaming").
- fire_ask: streaming_route=Main, live_turn=Some(Turn::new(Main)). cmd_btw: after sidecar creation, streaming_route=Btw, live_turn=Some(Turn::new(Btw)). cmd_cancel: matches streaming_route to pick session to cancel.
- render.rs renderers are already multi-turn-ready (render_turn takes label+color+items; pure). render:: tests = 11; total mu-solo tests = 32.

== PHASE 2 CHANGES ==
1. MODEL: replace `live_turn: Option<Turn>` with an ordered per-session map. RECOMMEND `live_turns: Vec<Turn>` with a new `session_id: String` field on Turn (avoids adding the indexmap dep; ≤2 concurrent turns so linear scan by sid is trivial; Vec order = start order = stable render/commit order). Helper: `fn live_turn_mut(&mut self, sid: &str) -> &mut Turn` that finds-or-creates (push) the turn for sid. Turn::new becomes `Turn::new(session_id, route)`.

2. ROUTE FROM SID, not the global streaming_route. Add `fn route_for_sid(&self, sid: &str) -> TurnRoute` (sid==self.session_id -> Main; sid==sidecar -> Btw). Every handler arm uses sid (already in scope at top of handle_notification) to get/create the right turn and its route. This removes the ambiguity the single streaming_route can't handle with two concurrent turns.

3. handle_notification arms: text_delta/finalized/tool_started/tool_completed -> operate on `self.live_turn_mut(sid)` instead of the single field. done|error -> remove + commit the turn for THIS sid only (find index by sid, remove, commit it); leave other in-flight turns alone. (sid is "" for some session-less notifications — keep the existing drop/Main-fallback behavior; don't create empty turns from provider_status.)

4. RELAX /btw GUARD: delete the `if self.live_turn.is_some()` block in cmd_btw so a sidecar turn can start mid-main-stream. cmd_btw creates the Btw turn keyed by the sidecar session_id. KEEP the magenta "assistant ⋅ btw" / "you ⋅ btw" labels — that route-colored framing is the visual separation between the two concurrent streams (no mux/panes; mu-6nuq deferred).

5. OTHER GUARDS: Ctrl-C and cmd_skill currently check live_turn.is_some() -> change to `!self.live_turns.is_empty()`. DECISION NEEDED: should /skill stay blocked while ANY turn streams? /skill injects context into a NEW main ask, which collides with an in-flight MAIN turn — recommend: /skill blocked only if a MAIN turn is in flight (not merely a btw sidecar). cmd_btw should NOT be blocked by an in-flight btw — but DO guard against a second concurrent btw if the sidecar is single (one btw at a time): block if a Btw turn is already live.

6. render_viewport: render EACH in-flight turn (Vec order = start order), each as its own open block (render_turn, no closer), route-colored. For ≤2 turns, concatenate all turn renders then tail_truncate the combined block to the same budget (simplest; acceptable that the top turn's header can scroll off under heavy load). If you want both headers always visible, split the budget per turn instead — note the trade-off. focus_mode still suppresses ALL previews.

7. streaming_route FATE: with route now derived per-sid, the single streaming_route is redundant for routing. cmd_cancel still needs a target: DECISION — cancel the MAIN turn by default (most intuitive), or cancel all in-flight. Recommend: /cancel cancels the main session's turn if one is live, else the btw. Either remove streaming_route and derive, or keep it only as "primary turn route" for /cancel. Don't leave it half-wired.

== WATCH-FORS / RISKS ==
- Commit ordering: if btw finishes before main, btw's block lands in scrollback ABOVE the still-streaming main (finish-order, not start-order). That's acceptable and chronological-by-finish; the original design note about "start-timestamp order" refers to LIVE render order (keep main above btw consistently), not scrollback. Don't try to reorder committed scrollback.
- Don't spuriously create empty turns for session-less / provider_status events. Only create on first content event (delta/tool).
- Empty-turn-on-done per session: keep the "(turn ended, no output)" / error path, scoped to the finishing sid.
- /cancel ambiguity (see #7). Ctrl-C cancel likewise needs a target policy.
- sidecar_session_id is created lazily in cmd_btw; ensure route_for_sid handles the sid before/after creation.

== VERIFY ==
- cargo build -p mu-solo && cargo clippy -p mu-solo && cargo test -p mu-solo (extend render tests for 2 concurrent turns; keep the tail-invariant per turn).
- Visual (thaddeus_user, live gpt-5.5): start a main turn, fire /btw mid-stream, confirm BOTH stream live (white main block + magenta btw block) and each commits to scrollback on its own done. Confirm /cancel + Ctrl-C target sanely. Then describe the change + advance main (direct push, branch-protection bypass — proper PR is a future cleanup).

Foundation for Phase 3 (nested collapsible child events) is unaffected by these changes.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
DECOMPOSITION (mu-mlcg, 2026-05-31). Terrain-checked the current mu-solo against the 5-phase design (the 2026-05-28 design notes predate the code).

Phase 1 (structured Turn/TurnItem model + pure renderers in render.rs + commit-on-finalize): ALREADY DONE on main — Turn{route,items} (app.rs:121-159), enum TurnItem (render.rs:124-138), render_turn/render_turn_item/render_tool_result + 9 tests, commit-on-finalize via insert_before only on done/error (app.rs:2028-2103); the old incremental fields (streaming_text/header_open/chars_emitted/pending_close) are gone. render.rs:109-119 documents this fixes mu-304g + mu-926g — so those two bugs do NOT manifest in mu-solo (operator: close them if mu-tui's F3 path is considered superseded by mu-solo; left open since they were filed against mu-tui).

Remaining work, filed as right-sized sub-beads (shared contract = the Turn model, already landed, so these build on it):
- mu-hsyt — Phase 2: per-session Turn map + concurrent /btw streaming (demux already routes by session_id; in-flight state is still a single Option<TurnRoute>, /btw still guarded). blockedBy: none.
- mu-jnbh — Phase 3: nested collapsible child events (Btw/tool-call as inline child). blockedBy mu-hsyt.
- mu-33o1 — Phase 4: provenance labels (session:provider:model; folds mu-za0u). Independent (self-contained header change).
- mu-p5pd — Phase 5: collapse/expand ratatui overlay (children + long tool output). blockedBy mu-jnbh.

Each is one-module-ish, independently testable, ~1-2h. mu-d04a now blockedBy all four (it's the umbrella). Related bug mu-wf5w (missing done-terminus) is a distinct daemon/finalize race — linked, not folded.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text:
OPERATOR REQUIREMENTS (dictated 2026-06-06 evening; supersede any implied design doctrine — explicitly 'no doctrine, not sure it works' re both the parked buffered impl urmmluyq AND the concurrent spec in mu-hsyt; requirements over implementation):

THE FAILURE BEING SOLVED: operator sends a refinement ('you don't need to worry about X') WHILE the model is processing the previous message. Wire ordering delivers it after the response, so the model misreads concurrent steering as a post-hoc verdict and redoes work that already accounted for it. The damage is stale-correction over-reaction, not ordering.

R1 — dispatch order is inviolate. A message precedes the provider calls it feeds. Never re-insert a mid-stream message before the response it didn't feed: temporally correct, causally a lie.

R2 — both truths persist. The event log records arrival time (already does). Projections must preserve BOTH where the message sat in time AND which provider calls it did/didn't feed. Two questions, two honest answers.

R3 — concurrency annotated in-band. When assembling the next provider call, a message that arrived mid-generation is marked 'authored while the previous response was streaming — author had not seen it'. Converts model interpretation from verdict-on-output to steering-that-may-already-be-satisfied. ContextAssembly-layer change; event log already has the timestamps.

R4 — rendering is free. Buffered (urmmluyq, parked) vs concurrent (mu-hsyt) is UI policy; neither satisfies nor violates R1-R3. Semantics live in the projection, not the viewport.

R5 — primitive taxonomy (operator history with claude-code /btw): he used /btw heavily in week 1-2 then never again — wanted '/advise_current_prompt', got '/spawn_independent_claude_to_ask_something_it_wont_remember', which has NO known use-case for him. Two axes: context-inherited-in x exchange-contributes-back. steer = current/yes (the real /btw intent; R1-R3 govern it). fork/spawn = snapshot/no (honest divergence; the multi-session shared-starting-context future; relates to session-identity spec's /rewind=fork). fresh session = none/no (his parallel-work pattern today). The discarded-sidechain quadrant (current-ish/no) is the trap — tempting, contributes nothing. mu's /btw should be the STEER primitive; a context-inheriting spawn is a separate primitive (/fork or /spawn), not a /btw variant.

Behavioral counterpart recorded in agent memory bf5b1b45 (reconcile-before-redo: check whether delivered work already satisfies a correction before mutating).

title: t4c discovery-quality: ranking layer (chain/neighborhood + tombstone + embeddings)
description: Add the discovery-QUALITY layer to t4c (crates/t4c, branch t4c-ranking off t4c-forward-build). Motivated by the mu-d33g cold-dogfood: token-overlap ranker + coarse catalog gave confident-WRONG find hits. Design (2026-05-29 conversation): (1) config grammar - a slot is either chain:[a,b,c] (interchangeable -> collapse to first-available, tombstone rest) OR distinct neighborhood entries (related but NOT interchangeable -> keep all, embeddings disambiguate); (2) discover resolves preference x availability STATICALLY and tombstones (3-state active/absent/superseded), then embeds the resolved set + caches vectors; (3) Embedder trait (FakeEmbedder deterministic + ConfigEmbedder reading ~/.config/agent/config.toml -> qwen3 endpoint); (4) SemanticRanker = cosine over cached vectors wired into find, preference baked at resolve NOT a runtime weight; (5) benchmark intent-sets (diff/compression/search, known answers) scored deterministically via FakeEmbedder. Small corpus => embed-all + brute cosine, no vector DB. usage-prior DEFERRED. STOP at the real-embedder re-dogfood + the judgment: does the blend make find earn its front-door role or concede to help/run. Worklist: br list --label goal:2026-05-29:SELF
close_reason: done
